bead.title: Make route-time local liveness nonblocking for autorouting
bead.description:
A dead local OpenAI-compatible provider (example: grendel rapid-mlx at http://grendel:8000/v1) can still delay or poison unpinned route selection before the routing engine chooses a healthy subscription harness. The current shared service path calls buildRoutingInputsWithCatalog(..., RefreshIfStale), which synchronously calls refreshLocalHealthForRouting and synchronous model snapshot refresh before scoring. This affects ResolveRoute directly, Execute via resolveExecuteRouteWithEngine, route-status, and override telemetry's stripped ResolveRoute call.

Required contract: local/fixed provider liveness and model discovery must be cached signals on the route hot path. Stale or missing local-provider facts may request a coordinated asynchronous refresh, but they must not block unpinned autorouting or explicit non-local harness selection. Provider contact failures must be recorded as route evidence/cooldown when observed, but failure to contact a local provider must not prevent selection of another eligible harness/provider. If every candidate is unavailable, routing must still fail with a typed no-viable-provider style error rather than hanging or aborting the process.

In-scope files:
- service_routing.go
- service_aliveness.go
- service_execute.go
- service_override.go only if context propagation requires it
- internal/modelsnapshot/types.go and internal/modelregistry/assemble.go comments if the freshness contract text is stale
- service_aliveness_test.go and service_routing_test.go for regression coverage
- docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-model-routing.md and/or docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-012-per-source-on-disk-cache.md to align the spec with the nonblocking route hot path
- CHANGELOG.md

Out-of-scope:
- DDx retry policy changes
- provider config schema changes
- changing explicit provider pins to silently fall back to a different provider
- requiring a DDx daemon for correctness
bead.acceptance:
1. A regression test proves ResolveRoute with a stale/dead local provider and an eligible subscription harness returns the subscription route quickly without synchronously invoking the local aliveness prober or /v1/models refresh.
2. A regression test proves Execute's under-specified route path uses the caller context and the same nonblocking ResolveRoute behavior; a blocked local aliveness prober cannot stall dispatch to an eligible explicit/implicit subscription harness.
3. Existing probe evidence still gates known-dead local providers: a fresh failed ProbeStore record rejects the local candidate with endpoint_unreachable while a healthy alternate is selected.
4. Specs/docs explicitly state that route hot paths are cache-first and may request async refresh, while explicit refresh surfaces may block.
5. `go test ./... -run 'TestResolveRoute_.*Local|TestResolveRouteRefreshesStaleSnapshot|TestExecute_.*Route|TestResolveRouteSnapshotFreshCacheSkipsDiscoveryProbe' -count=1` passes.
6. `go test ./... -count=1` passes.
